mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, provides a favorable environment for equity valuations by reducing uncertainty and supporting consumer purchasing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%)
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat during periods of low and stable CPIAUCSL, or the correlation turns significantly negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals an economic downturn, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, which boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat when both RSAFS shows strong growth and FEDFUNDS is low, or the interaction term shows no significant predictive power (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures, leading to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive during periods of declining INDPRO and high CPIAUCSL, or the signal's direction flips in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level indicates weakening consumer and business confidence, which foreshadows broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where HOUST falls below 900k units over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as measured by PAYEMS, reflects a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are consistently negative or flat during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth, or the correlation turns significantly negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a Federal Reserve tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat when FEDFUNDS is rising and UNRATE is low, or the combined signal shows no significant negative predictive power (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) during periods of low inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a healthy, non-inflationary expansion, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative during periods of strong GDPC1 growth and low CPIAUCSL, or the signal's direction flips in this specific regime.
